title: Receive offers - Handle late or delayed offers | CanadaBuys

breadcrumbs.title: Apply the phased offer compliance process
Determine if a peer review is required

structure_blocks.heading: Buyer's Guide Receive offers - Handle late or delayed offers
heading_path: Buyer's Guide Receive offers - Handle late or delayed offers
structure_blocks.text: Handle late or delayed offers The guidelines below address offers against competitive solicitations of offers that are submitted after the closing date and time. They also address offers that may be delayed due to specific reasons, ensuring a fair, transparent, and equitable process for all offerors. Late and delayed offer submission The timely submission of offers is critical to maintaining the integrity of the procurement process. Below are the procedures for handling offers that do not meet the specified deadline: for all competitive solicitations of offers, the specified closing date and time outlined in the solicitation documents are firm offerors are responsible for ensuring their offer reaches the appropriate location on time the only acceptable evidence to show timely receipt of the offer at a specified offer receiving unit is the receipt issued by the specified offer receiving unit the only pieces of evidence relating to a delay in the Canada Post Corporate (CPC) system that are acceptable to PSPC are a: CPC cancellation date stamp CPC Priority Courier bill of lading CPC Xpresspost label (note: the evidence provided must also clearly indicate that the offer was sent before the solicitation closing date) the only piece of evidence relating to a delay in the CPC Connect service provided by the CPC system that is acceptable to PSPC is a CPC Connect service date and time record the evidence must be indicated in the CPC Connect conversation history and clearly show that the offer was sent before the solicitation closing date and time for competitive solicitations of offers, late offers delivered using methods other than CPC 's Connect service or other electronic delivery methods will be returned unless they qualify as delayed offers for all competitive solicitations of offers, offers submitted via CPC 's Connect service that are deemed late will result in the deletion of any related conversation initiated by offer receiving units concerning the late offer documentation of the transaction history for all late offers submitted using this service will be maintained Equipment calibration for timely submission verification To ensure the accuracy of official time-keeping equipment used to verify the timely submission of offers, offer receiving units must calibrate this equipment against the National Research Council (NRC) official time standard at least once every two working days.

raw_text:
Handle late or delayed offers The guidelines below address offers against competitive solicitations of offers that are submitted after the closing date and time. They also address offers that may be delayed due to specific reasons, ensuring a fair, transparent, and equitable process for all offerors. Late and delayed offer submission The timely submission of offers is critical to maintaining the integrity of the procurement process. Below are the procedures for handling offers that do not meet the specified deadline: for all competitive solicitations of offers, the specified closing date and time outlined in the solicitation documents are firm offerors are responsible for ensuring their offer reaches the appropriate location on time the only acceptable evidence to show timely receipt of the offer at a specified offer receiving unit is the receipt issued by the specified offer receiving unit the only pieces of evidence relating to a delay in the Canada Post Corporate (CPC) system that are acceptable to PSPC are a: CPC cancellation date stamp CPC Priority Courier bill of lading CPC Xpresspost label (note: the evidence provided must also clearly indicate that the offer was sent before the solicitation closing date) the only piece of evidence relating to a delay in the CPC Connect service provided by the CPC system that is acceptable to PSPC is a CPC Connect service date and time record the evidence must be indicated in the CPC Connect conversation history and clearly show that the offer was sent before the solicitation closing date and time for competitive solicitations of offers, late offers delivered using methods other than CPC 's Connect service or other electronic delivery methods will be returned unless they qualify as delayed offers for all competitive solicitations of offers, offers submitted via CPC 's Connect service that are deemed late will result in the deletion of any related conversation initiated by offer receiving units concerning the late offer documentation of the transaction history for all late offers submitted using this service will be maintained Equipment calibration for timely submission verification To ensure the accuracy of official time-keeping equipment used to verify the timely submission of offers, offer receiving units must calibrate this equipment against the National Research Council (NRC) official time standard at least once every two working days.

Handle late or delayed offers

The guidelines below address offers against competitive solicitations of offers that are submitted after the closing date and time. They also address offers that may be delayed due to specific reasons, ensuring a fair, transparent, and equitable process for all offerors.

Late and delayed offer submission

The timely submission of offers is critical to maintaining the integrity of the procurement process. Below are the procedures for handling offers that do not meet the specified deadline:

for all competitive solicitations of offers, the specified closing date and time outlined in the solicitation documents are firm

offerors are responsible for ensuring their offer reaches the appropriate location on time

the only acceptable evidence to show timely receipt of the offer at a specified offer receiving unit is the receipt issued by the specified offer receiving unit

the only pieces of evidence relating to a delay in the Canada Post Corporate (CPC) system that are acceptable to PSPC are a: CPC cancellation date stamp CPC Priority Courier bill of lading CPC Xpresspost label (note: the evidence provided must also clearly indicate that the offer was sent before the solicitation closing date)

CPC cancellation date stamp

CPC Priority Courier bill of lading

CPC Xpresspost label (note: the evidence provided must also clearly indicate that the offer was sent before the solicitation closing date)

the only piece of evidence relating to a delay in the CPC Connect service provided by the CPC system that is acceptable to PSPC is a CPC Connect service date and time record the evidence must be indicated in the CPC Connect conversation history and clearly show that the offer was sent before the solicitation closing date and time

the evidence must be indicated in the CPC Connect conversation history and clearly show that the offer was sent before the solicitation closing date and time

for competitive solicitations of offers, late offers delivered using methods other than CPC 's Connect service or other electronic delivery methods will be returned unless they qualify as delayed offers

for all competitive solicitations of offers, offers submitted via CPC 's Connect service that are deemed late will result in the deletion of any related conversation initiated by offer receiving units concerning the late offer documentation of the transaction history for all late offers submitted using this service will be maintained

documentation of the transaction history for all late offers submitted using this service will be maintained

Equipment calibration for timely submission verification

To ensure the accuracy of official time-keeping equipment used to verify the timely submission of offers, offer receiving units must calibrate this equipment against the National Research Council (NRC) official time standard at least once every two working days.
